preamble: The shift from [e] to [y] involves vowel rounding, where a mid-front unrounded vowel gains lip rounding. This often occurs as part of a complex chain shift or via intermediate diphthongs, notably seen in the history of Greek.

examples.from: η ēta [eː] (Classical Greek long mid front)
examples.note: In the iotacism process, Classical Greek long [eː] (eta) passed through a rounded stage [yː] before merging with [iː] in Modern Greek. Cf. also οι [oi] → [øː] → [yː] → [i].
